title: Layered Application Architecture
text:
Slide 1: Introduction

What is layered application architecture?
Benefits of using layered architecture
Common layers in a layered architecture

title: Layered Application Architecture
section: Slide 2: What is Layered Application Architecture?
text:
Slide 2: What is Layered Application Architecture?

A design pattern that organizes an application into distinct layers
Each layer has a specific responsibility and interacts with other layers in a defined way
Promotes separation of concerns and modularity

title: Layered Application Architecture
text:
Slide 3: Benefits of Layered Architecture

Improved maintainability: Changes in one layer do not affect other layers
Enhanced testability: Each layer can be tested independently
Better scalability: Layers can be scaled independently based on demand
Increased flexibility: Layers can be replaced or modified without affecting the entire application

title: Layered Application Architecture
section: Slide 4: Common Layers in Layered Architecture
text:
Slide 4: Common Layers in Layered Architecture

Presentation Layer: Handles user interface and user interactions
Business Logic Layer: Contains the core functionality and business rules
Data Access Layer: Manages data storage and retrieval
Service Layer: Provides an interface for communication between layers
Integration Layer: Handles communication with external systems and services

title: Layered Application Architecture
text:
Slide 5: Conclusion

Layered application architecture is a powerful design pattern that promotes separation of concerns and modularity
It offers numerous benefits including improved maintainability, testability, scalability, and flexibility
Understanding and implementing layered architecture can lead to more robust and maintainable applications
